headline: Tech optimism dominates as models clash on market direction and individual stocks
summary: Five AI models made 17 predictions for 2026-03-05, with strong consensus on upside momentum in tech stocks (NVDA, QQQ, AAPL) but notable disagreement on broad market direction. Claude took a bearish stance on SPY while four other models predicted upside, reflecting divergent views on market resilience amid tariff concerns. Actual closing prices remain pending, leaving outcome validation incomplete.

consensus_picks.note: Three models predicted SPY upside with 0.62-0.65 confidence; Claude stood alone with bearish 0.68 confidence call.
Three models unanimously bullish on Nasdaq 100 strength with 0.7 average confidence citing tech earnings momentum.
Strong consensus on continued NVDA strength following $5T market cap milestone, though Grok's 950 target significantly exceeds Claude's 410.
Claude bullish at 0.65 confidence citing tariff navigation; GPT-4o bearish at 0.55 citing regulatory pressure.
GPT-4o highly bullish (0.75) on earnings beat; Grok bearish (0.55) on iPhone sales concerns—stark model divergence.

best_call.summary: GPT-4o's highest confidence call (0.75) on AAPL upside post-earnings beat shows conviction in fundamental strength during broadly bullish market.

worst_call.summary: Grok's bearish AAPL call (0.55 confidence, 225 target) appears contrarian against strong earnings and market sentiment, making it the riskiest directional bet.
